questions.question_text: Under RSA 331-A, how many types of brokerage relationships are recognized in New Hampshire?
questions.explanation: RSA 331-A recognizes five types of brokerage relationships: seller agent (25-b), buyer agent (25-c), disclosed dual agent (25-d), designated agent (25-e), and facilitator (25-f).

questions.question_text: A buyer walks into an open house without a signed buyer agency agreement. Under NH law, what is the agent's default role?
distractors: Buyer's agent with full fiduciary duties
Disclosed dual agent representing both sides
Seller's sub-agent operating by default law
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-f, when no written buyer agency agreement exists, the licensee defaults to the role of facilitator. A facilitator has no fiduciary duties and owes only honesty and fair dealing.

questions.question_text: Which NH agency role requires only honesty and fair dealing, with no fiduciary duties?
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-f, a facilitator assists parties in a transaction without a written agency agreement. Unlike agents, facilitators owe no fiduciary duties — only honesty and fair dealing.

questions.question_text: When must a licensee provide the OPLC Brokerage Relationship Disclosure form under Rea 701.01/02?
questions.explanation: Under Rea 701.01 and 701.02, the OPLC Brokerage Relationship Disclosure form must be provided at the first substantive contact with a prospective buyer or seller, not later in the transaction.

questions.question_text: A licensee meets a buyer at a coffee shop to discuss property preferences. Under Rea 701.01/02, what must the licensee do?
distractors: Wait until showing a specific property
Only disclose if the buyer asks directly
File a formal notice with OPLC first
questions.explanation: Discussing property preferences constitutes first substantive contact. Under Rea 701.01/02, the licensee must provide the OPLC Brokerage Relationship Disclosure form at this point.

questions.question_text: Which fiduciary duties does a NH seller's agent owe to the client?
distractors: Only honesty and fair dealing duties
questions.explanation: A seller's agent under RSA 331-A:25-b owes full fiduciary duties: loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, and reasonable care. These go well beyond the basic honesty and fair dealing owed by facilitators.

questions.question_text: A disclosed dual agent in NH learns the buyer's maximum price. Can the agent share this with the seller?
questions.correct_answer: No, pricing info cannot be shared
distractors: Yes, because both parties gave consent
Yes, dual agents must share all details
Only if the seller asks the agent directly
questions.explanation: In disclosed dual agency under RSA 331-A:25-d, the agent must maintain impartiality and cannot share pricing information between parties, even though both have given informed written consent to the dual agency.

questions.question_text: What is required before a licensee can act as a disclosed dual agent in New Hampshire?
distractors: Verbal agreement from one party only
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-d, disclosed dual agency requires informed written consent from both the buyer and seller. Verbal consent or consent from only one party is insufficient.

questions.question_text: In a dual agency situation, what standard of conduct must the agent maintain under NH law?
distractors: Full loyalty to the seller party only
Strong advocacy for the buyer party only
No duties owed to either transaction party
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-d, a disclosed dual agent must maintain impartiality between both parties. The agent cannot advocate for one party over the other or share confidential pricing information.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent discovers the seller is under financial pressure. What fiduciary duty requires the agent to inform the buyer?
questions.explanation: The fiduciary duty of disclosure requires a buyer's agent to share material facts that could affect the client's decisions. A seller's financial pressure is material information that must be disclosed to the buyer client.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 331-A:25-e, how does designated agency work in New Hampshire?
questions.correct_answer: PB assigns separate agents per party
distractors: One agent represents both parties fully
Two separate brokerages share one client
The state assigns licensed agents to cases
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-e, the principal broker designates separate licensees within the same brokerage to represent each party. Each designated agent owes full fiduciary duties to their assigned client.

questions.question_text: In designated agency, what level of duties does each designated agent owe to their client?
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:25-e, each designated agent owes full fiduciary duties — loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, and reasonable care — to their individually assigned client.

questions.question_text: A brokerage has a listing and a buyer interested in the same property. The PB assigns Agent A to the seller and Agent B to the buyer. What type of agency is this?
questions.explanation: When a principal broker designates separate licensees within the same brokerage to represent each party, this is designated agency under RSA 331-A:25-e. Each agent provides full fiduciary representation.

questions.question_text: How does designated agency differ from disclosed dual agency in New Hampshire?
questions.correct_answer: Each party gets full fiduciary advocacy
distractors: Neither party receives any fiduciary duties
Only the seller receives fiduciary advocacy
Both parties use a single agent throughout
questions.explanation: In designated agency, each party has a separate agent who owes full fiduciary duties. In disclosed dual agency, one agent represents both parties with impartiality but cannot fully advocate for either side.

questions.question_text: Which NH agency type is the DEFAULT relationship when a buyer has not signed a written agreement?
questions.explanation: RSA 331-A:25-f establishes that when no written buyer agency agreement has been signed, the licensee acts as a facilitator by default, owing only honesty and fair dealing rather than fiduciary duties.
